text:
Once upon a time, there was a little pig who loved going for baths. She would just roll in mud, splashing around delightfully.

On a sunny day, after playing all morning with her friends, she decided she wanted a big muddy bath. She snuck into the muddy puddle corner near the field.

As she went under, the mud hugged her body warmly. She loved it so much she didn't want to come out. She made little pigsqueal noises while enjoying the comfort and coolness.

Then, she remembered something important. She had eaten a big piece of garlic that morning. Being exposed to garlic was terrible for pigs. She quickly climbed out and ran toward the safety of the pond.

After a quick rinse to wash away the garlic scent, she looked herself over. She was indeed a muddy little pig. But she was also happy. Cleaning didn't have to mean anything big or important.

She had the best bathtub mud ever.

That night, as she lay there
